title: zieria pilosa var. pauciflora
intro: Zieria pilosa var. pauciflora, also known as the few-flowered zieria, is a rare and endemic plant species found in the eastern coast of Australia. It is a shrub-like plant with medicinal properties, traditionally used by the Indigenous Australians to treat various health conditions. The plant is said to have antimicrobial, antifungal, and anti-inflammatory properties, which contribute to its potential health benefits, including wound healing, and relief from pain and fever. Zieria pilosa var. pauciflora can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures for medicinal purposes, although its use should be approached with caution and under the guidance of a qualified healthcare professional due to the limited research available.
